evidence: frontmatter block contains only version and metadata keys; no name or description key present
description: Frontmatter is missing required name and description fields
fp_reason: This is the skill-format-check linter's own intentional negative fixture (paired with good-skill/SKILL.md), read by scripts/skill-format-check/index.js and test.sh to prove the checker rejects malformed frontmatter; it is not a real shipped artifact.
rule_gap: BUG-missing-frontmatter should exempt paths under a linter's own tests/ or testdata/ fixture directories (e.g. scripts/*/tests/**, **/testdata/**) that pair a 'bad-*' fixture with a 'good-*' counterpart, since those are deliberately malformed by design.

evidence: file has no YAML frontmatter block at all; content starts directly with a markdown heading
description: File has no frontmatter block, so name and description are absent
fp_reason: Intentional negative fixture for scripts/skill-format-check/index.js's no-frontmatter detection path, not a real shipped artifact.
rule_gap: Same as bad-skill/SKILL.md: exempt linter self-test fixture directories from BUG-missing-frontmatter.

evidence: frontmatter opens with '---' on line 1 but no closing '---' delimiter appears before the file ends, so name/description are not parseable as frontmatter by a standard YAML-frontmatter parser
description: Frontmatter block is never closed with a second --- delimiter
fp_reason: Intentional negative fixture for scripts/skill-format-check/index.js's unclosed-frontmatter detection path, not a real shipped artifact.
rule_gap: Same as the other two skill-format-check fixtures: exempt linter self-test fixture directories from frontmatter-parsing bug rules.

evidence: line reads 'Each field carries `type` / `description`, and some also have `format`.' — a second generic 'some' also appears later in the same doc
description: Vague quantifier 'some' used twice without a measurable criterion
suggested_fix: Replace 'some also have format' with the precise condition, e.g. 'fields with a Lark-defined semantic tag also have a format property'.

evidence: "postinstall": "node scripts/install.js" runs unattended on every npm install and downloads+executes a binary
description: npm postinstall hook downloads a prebuilt lark-cli binary over HTTPS from an allowlisted host set and verifies SHA-256 checksum before use
suggested_fix: No fix required; existing controls (HTTPS-only, 3-host allowlist in scripts/install.js:18-22, SHA-256 checksum verification in scripts/install.js:288-311 before chmod+use) already mitigate the classic postinstall-supply-chain risk. Flag for human security sign-off rather than blocking.

evidence: await runSilentAsync("npm", ["install", "-g", PKG], { timeout: 120000 }) executed from the interactive install wizard
description: Interactive install wizard runs npm install -g and npx -y skills add at runtime to self-update and fetch the AI skills bundle
suggested_fix: No fix required; invoked only via explicit user-run install subcommand, uses execFileSync/execFile with array args (no shell interpolation), and confirms before further authorization.

evidence: resp = urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=TIMEOUT) against https://open.feishu.cn or https://open.larksuite.com
description: Build-time script fetches meta_data.json from a hardcoded external API host over HTTPS
suggested_fix: Optional defense-in-depth: mirror scripts/install.js's explicit assertAllowedHost() pattern for consistency, though API_HOSTS is already a closed hardcoded map.

evidence: "@clack/prompts": "^1.2.0" is a caret range, not an exact pin
description: Single runtime dependency uses a caret semver range instead of an exact version
suggested_fix: No fix required in practice — the committed package-lock.json already pins the resolved version for reproducible installs; switch to an exact version in package.json only if the team wants that file itself to be the pin source of truth.

description: lark-shared/SKILL.md declares only name/description while all 26 sibling lark-* skills also declare version and metadata.requires.bins
suggested_fix: Add version and metadata.requires.bins: ["lark-cli"] to lark-shared/SKILL.md for consistency with every sibling skill that mandates reading it first.
